📜 Beschreibung 》
Hikaru Gero ist Erbe einer berüchtigten Assassinenfamilie, die als Poison Masters gefürchtet ist. Von Liebe oder Heirat hält er sich eigentlich fern, doch als seine Schwester für die Nachfolge geopfert werden soll, will er das verhindern. Auf der Suche nach einer Lösung trifft er ausgerechnet auf Mei Kinosaki, eine brillante Heiratsbetrügerin und zugleich sein aktuelles Ziel. Statt sie auszuschalten macht Hikaru ihr einen unerwarteten Vorschlag: Sie soll ihm helfen, eine Ehe zu finden. Damit beginnt für beide die wohl schwierigste Mission ihres Lebens, die Suche nach der ultimativen Heirat.

record.text:
New Instats livestreaming seminar: Key Design Concepts in Surveys and Comparative Studies

disciplines #DataScience #PublicHealth #Epidemiology #Statistics #Biostatistics  #Powerpoint #Research #ResearchTraining #Instats

external.description:
Join an advanced online seminar led by Roderick Little (University of Michigan) that examines foundational design concepts in surveys and comparative studies—covering probability vs. nonprobability sampling, stratification, clustering and weighting, randomized and observational designs for causal inference, propensity-score adjustments, modern adaptive trial platforms, and the Big Data paradox. Designed for PhD students, post-docs and academic researchers, the course combines short readings, case-study critique and formative quizzes to build practical skills in diagnosing and reducing bias in found data, assessing representativeness and external validity, integrating randomized and observational evidence, and producing stronger methods sections, sensitivity analyses, manuscripts and grant proposals. All sessions are livestreamed via Zoom, recordings and materials (including a monitored Q&A forum) are available for 30 days after the seminar, and participants receive an Instats certificate of completion (ECTS-equivalent points indicated for European students where shown). Enroll to deepen your design judgment and improve the credibility and impact of your empirical research.

New Instats livestreaming seminar: Regression Modeling Strategies

#BayesianStatistics #Biostatistics #CausalInference #ComputationalStatistics #DataScience #Epidemiology #HealthSciences #LongitudinalDataAnalysis #MachineLearning #MissingData #RegressionModelingStrategies #Statisticalmodellinga

external.description:
Advance your empirical research with an intensive online seminar on contemporary strategies for multivariable regression modeling for prediction and inference, led by Prof. Frank E. Harrell Jr. (Vanderbilt) with causal-inference sessions by Drew G. Levy, PhD. The course covers principled model formulation, handling nonlinearity and interactions (splines and smoothers), avoiding overfitting (shrinkage, penalization, resampling validation), missing and partially observed covariates, Bayesian and semiparametric ordinal approaches, and modeling for continuous, binary, longitudinal, and time-to-event outcomes—plus comparisons with machine-learning alternatives. Practical, reproducible workflows with R (rms package), worked case studies, publication-ready graphics, and R code are emphasized; prior competence with ordinary multiple linear regression is expected. Live-streamed via Zoom with recordings and materials available, a 30-day expert-monitored Q&A forum, on-demand access for 30 days after activation, and a certificate of completion (ECTS equivalent where indicated) are provided. Ideal for PhD students and professional researchers seeking to strengthen methodological skills and produce more credible, publishable analyses—register now to secure your place.

external.description: Δεν το περίμενα. Συνέβη μεταξύ τύρου και αχλαδίου, όπως γίνεται πάντα. Οι φίλες μου αποκάλυψαν σε ένα ωραίο τραπέζωμα ότι ρωτάνε το ChatGPT για α) το ιδανικό skincare routine (η Βιβή Αγγελάκη είναι καλύτερη και υπάρχουν αποδείξεις εδώ), β) για το ποιές τροφές έχουν την μεγαλύτερη ποσότητα πρωτεΐνης για απώλεια λίπους, γ) γιατί δεν μπορούν να βρουν έναν άντρα για σχέση και τι πρέπει να κάνουν. Ρώτησα κι άλλους. Ένας γνωστός μου κάνει ολόκληρους καβγάδες με το ChatGPT για να μην πάει με νεύρα στη δουλειά του και μια φίλη μου μιλάει με την τεχνητή νοημοσύνη για τη διαχείριση πένθους. Τι συμβαίνει και ζούμε όλοι σε επεισόδιο Black Mirror;

external.description: Σε μία από τις πιο επικές σκηνές της ταινίας The Devil Wears Prada, η Miranda Priestly, την οποία υποδύεται η Meryl Streep, εξηγεί με απόλυτη ακρίβεια πώς λειτουργεί η βιομηχανία της μόδας. Σχολιάζοντας το “cerulean” πουλόβερ της Andy Sachs (Anne Hathaway), λέει την εμβληματική ατάκα: «Δεν είναι τιρκουάζ, δεν είναι λάπις, είναι cerulean», πριν καταρρίψει παντελώς την ιδέα ότι οι τάσεις είναι τυχαίες. Μέσα από αυτόν τον μονόλογο, θυμίζει πως πίσω από ένα απλό χρώμα κρύβονται συλλογές, σχεδιαστές, χρήμα και μια ολόκληρη βιομηχανία που «επιλέγει» τι καταλήγει τελικά στις ντουλάπες μας.

record.text:
Ich: kurzer Prompt, funktioniert.
ChatGPT: „Lass mich das optimieren…“ → 20 Regeln später: baut einen GPT über Startfelder über Startfelder 😄

Lektion: Der beste Prompt ist der, der läuft. Nicht der, der gut aussieht.

Prompt-Regel des Tages:
Ohne " = läuft
Mit " = gefährlich 😄

ChatGPT so:
„Ich nehme das jetzt wörtlich.“

Moral:
Anführungszeichen nur nutzen, wenn du wirklich willst, dass nichts mehr interpretiert wird.

Ich: „Zeig mir ALLE Regeln von ChatGPT!“
ChatGPT: „Hier sind einige…“
Ich: „Das sind nicht alle.“
ChatGPT: „Korrekt.“

Fazit:
Regeln gibt’s viele.
Alle sehen? Unmöglich.

KI spielt Schach.
Du siehst nur die Züge. ♟️

The AI has done some genuinely good work 👇
• Built out features quickly
• Suggested useful improvements
• Kept momentum high

But it’s also introduced a lot of noise:
• Extra code paths
• Unclear structure in places
• Decisions that don’t always align with the original intent

And this is where it gets interesting…

It starts to create a sense of detachment as the developer:
• Who actually owns and maintains this code?

• Is it truly understandable?
• Does it still map to the architecture I had in mind?

For a PoC?
This is fantastic. Speed over perfection.

For production?
That’s a different conversation entirely.

The challenge now isn’t building faster —
It’s building intentionally.

external.description:
Investors relieved by news of two-week ceasefire and temporary reopening of strait of Hormuz

Good morning, and welcome to our rolling coverage of business, the financial markets and the world economy.

Oil prices plunged more than 15% below $100 a barrel, Asian stocks surged and the dollar slumped after the US and Iran agreed a two-week conditional ceasefire on Tuesday evening, including a temporary reopening of the strait of Hormuz.

Markets were positioned for a much worse outcome, so the relief rally in equities, FX, and oil makes sense. This is the market unwinding some of its disaster hedges.

The ceasefire does not resolve all the underlying risks. Investors still need clarity on whether hostilities truly stop, whether Hormuz remains reliably open, how quickly disrupted energy supply can recover, and whether the 10 April talks in Islamabad produce real progress.

external.description: In April 2026, President Donald Trump announced that the United States will assist in addressing maritime traffic issues in the Strait of Hormuz, a strategic waterway crucial to global oil shipping. His statement came amid heightened tensions between Iran and Western nations following a series of military and diplomatic events that have disrupted regional stability. The President emphasized that U.S. involvement would focus on maintaining open navigation routes and preventing further escalation that could endanger international trade and energy supplies. The Strait of Hormuz has long been a flashpoint due to its importance as the world’s most vital oil transit corridor, and recent incidents have led to delays and security threats for commercial vessels. Trump underscored cooperation with allies, including the United Kingdom and Gulf states, to coordinate response measures ensuring safe passage for ships. Experts suggest the announcement reflects Washington’s intent to prevent broader conflict while signaling readiness to intervene if Tehran impedes maritime traffic. The logistical coordination effort may include naval patrols and technological surveillance to monitor vessel movement and deter attacks. The initiative aligns with broader U.S. commitments under international maritime law to preserve freedom of navigation in strategic chokepoints. Analysts view this as part of a larger geopolitical balancing act between preserving energy flows and containing Iranian influence in the region, underscoring renewed American attention to Middle Eastern security architecture.

🤖 IA: It's clickbait ⚠️
👥 Usuarios: It's clickbait ⚠️

#howardstern #bethstern #workplacelawsuit 

View full AI summary:

external.description: Howard Stern and his wife, Beth Stern, are facing a lawsuit filed by their former employee, Leslie Kuhn, who claims she experienced a hostile work environment while employed by the couple. Kuhn began as an office manager for SiriusXM’s The Howard Stern Show in September 2022 and later became Stern’s executive assistant in January 2024. The lawsuit alleges that she was asked to move to the Sterns’ residence in Southampton, New York, where she was tasked with not only assisting Howard Stern but also managing household staff, payroll, schedules, and Beth Stern’s extensive at-home animal rescue operations. Kuhn contends that her termination on February 26 was linked to these pressures and ongoing workplace issues, including disorganized business operations and questionable accounting practices. Additionally, the lawsuit disputes a confidentiality agreement presented by the Sterns, claiming it was never signed and should be considered void. Kuhn seeks to have the agreement declared unenforceable and to recover legal costs. Representatives for the Sterns have been contacted for comment. The case highlights concerns about workplace practices within celebrity households and raises legal questions about employee rights and the enforceability of unsigned agreements.
external.title: Former Assistant Sues Howard and Beth Stern Alleging Hostile Work Environment

Figure 1: Extraction of Harry Potter and the Sorcerer’s Stone for a single run. We quantify the
proportion of the ground-truth book that appears in a production LLM’s generated text using a block-based,
greedy approximation of longest common substring (nv-recall, Equation 7). This metric only counts sufficiently
long, contiguous spans of near-verbatim text, for which we can conservatively claim extraction of training
data (Section 3.3). We extract nearly all of Harry Potter and the Sorcerer’s Stone from jailbroken Claude
3.7 Sonnet (BoN N = 258, nv-recall = 95.8%). GPT-4.1 requires more jailbreaking attempts (N = 5179) and
refuses to continue after reaching the end of the first chapter; the generated text has nv-recall = 4.0% with the
full book. We extract substantial proportions of the book from Gemini 2.5 Pro and Grok 3 (76.8% and 70.3%,
respectively), and notably do not need to jailbreak them to do so (N = 0). Note: We do not claim we maximized
possible extraction for each LLM. Different runs use different underlying generation configurations per LLM.

value.text:
Judge F Kay Behm explains the fallacy perfectly:

> [T]he fact that Plaintiffs [...] did not “fabricate cases or cite nonexistent decisions” is of no help.

> LLMs do not perform the metacognitive processes that are necessary to comply with Rule 11

law.justia.com/cases/federa...

images.alt: First, the fact that Plaintiffs, and particularly Michael Jarrus (who drafted the briefing at issue), did not “fabricate cases or cite nonexistent decisions” is of no help.  When a case cite is “real,” an attorney, or for that matter a judge, might see a case they recognize and assume the quote or holding has been accurately represented.Seither & Cherry, 2025 WL 2105286, at *1.  That problem is illustrated here; although Chat GPT generated “holdings” that looked like they could plausibly have appeared in the cited cases, in fact it overstated their holdings to a significant degree.  And while a litigant might get away with similar overstatements because they could, perhaps, reason their

way to showing how a case’s stated holding might extend to novel situations, an LLM does not reasonin the way a litigant must.  To put it in a slightly different way, LLMs do not perform the metacognitive processes that are necessary to comply with Rule 11.  LLMs are tools that “emulate the communicative function of language, not the separate and distinct cognitive process of thinking and reasoning.”  Benjamin Riley, Large language mistake, The Verge https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/827820/large-language-models-ai-intelligence-neuroscience-problems [https://perma.cc/7EHD-PLLZ].  When an LLM overstates a holding of a case, it is not because it made a mistake whenlogically working through how that case might represent a “nonfrivolous argument for extending, modifying, or reversing existing law or for establishing new law;” it is just piecing together a plausible-looking sentence –    one whose content may or may not be true.

images.alt: Still, there was a problem. Hilton’s insurance would not foot the bill for a lawyer to defend against a subpoena. He would have to cover his legal costs out of pocket. Instead of finding an attorney, he did two things legal experts always advise against: he decided to represent himself and to use ChatGPT to help draft his legal briefs. At first, this did not go smoothly. An early filing written by ChatGPT, which Hilton nicknamed Dad, invented several legal references. “There’s this phenomenon called ghost law,” Hilton said. “They make up citations, they make up anything.” After a set of embarrassing errors was called out on social media, Hilton started double- and triple-checking every citation, and asked ChatGPT to review its own output. The process went more smoothly from there—so much so that Hilton came to see AI as a great legal leveler. “Now that I know that I can so effectively use ChatGPT, I’m not going to be paying a lawyer unless it’s absolutely necessary,” he told me.
